title: Filipino Pork Sinigang Stew
description: A classic Filipino sour soup made with pork and various vegetables in a tangy broth.
instructions: Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Sear the pork pieces until browned on all sides.
Remove the pork from the pot and set aside.
Add more oil if necessary and sauté the onion and tomato until softened.
Add the sinigang mix, tamarind paste, and water to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Return the pork to the pot and cook for an additional 10 minutes, or until the meat is tender.
Add the chopped bok choy and green chili to the pot and cook until wilted.
Season the stew with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
